nameMeanings.name: Ernst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Kurt, Heinz, Helmut, Manfred, Reinhold, Ulrich, Fritz, Gunter, Hans, Heinrich.

German, Dutch, and Danish: from the personal name Ernst, which is most probably a byname from Middle High German and Middle Dutch ern(e)st ‘combat, serious business’. This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, and Czechia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname or artificial name from German ernst ‘earnest, serious’. Compare Ernest .

variants.name: Ernesti
meanings: German: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Ernst , using the genitive of its Latinized form Ernestus.

variants.name: Earnst
meanings: Americanized form of German, Dutch, and Jewish   Ernst  .

variants.name: Ernster
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Ernst  , from an inflectedform of the German adjective.

variants.name: Arnst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Achim, Kurt, Otto.
German: probably a variant of   Ernst  .

variants.name: Ernsting
meanings: German: patronymic from the personal name   Ernst  .

variants.name: Enslen
meanings: German: from a pet form of the personal name   Ernst  . This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Ernzen
meanings: Luxembourgish: probably a patronymic from the personal name   Ernst  .

variants.name: Ernest
meanings: French, Walloon, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Jewish, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Tanzania): from the personal name Ernest, a cognate or a variant of German, Dutch, and Jewish Ernst .
Americanized form of German, Dutch, Danish, and Jewish   Ernst  .
English: variant of Harness , by folk-etymological association with the vocabulary word earnest. Compare Earnest .

variants.name: Ernesto
meanings: Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, and Italian: from the personal name Ernesto (see Ernst ).

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

en.lifeSummary: Anna Ernst was born in 1556, in Baden. She married Michael Zimmermann on 11 August 1578, in Baden. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters.
